heroTitle: IB Tutor Support for Saket and South Delhi Families
heroSubtitle: Saket sits at a practical mid-point for South Delhi's international-school corridor, making it a natural hub for families seeking IB home tuition or an online backup when schedules tighten. IB Gram connects students here with tutors experienced across PYP, MYP and DP. Availability depends on subject, schedule, exact location, tutor fit and mode — we help you find the right match.
introSummary: Saket is a well-connected residential pocket of South Delhi, neighboured by areas such as Defence Colony, Hauz Khas and Greater Kailash. Families here often juggle demanding school timetables with commute realities, making personalised, flexible IB support — whether at home or online — a practical necessity rather than a luxury.

contentBlocks.heading: Why Saket Families Seek Dedicated IB Support
contentBlocks.body:
Saket occupies a layered position in Delhi's education landscape. As a dense residential area in South Delhi, it draws families who are often navigating the transition between Indian curriculum schools and international-programme schools, or who have already committed to an IB school and are finding the curriculum's demands steeper than anticipated. Unlike more centralised tutoring markets, families in Saket typically look for tutors who understand both the local school calendar and the specific IB assessment framework — not just subject knowledge alone.

The IB Diploma Programme in particular is known for compressing high academic rigour into a two-year window, with internal assessments, the Extended Essay and Theory of Knowledge running concurrently. Students in Saket who attend schools offering DP often describe a sense of time-pressure that general tuition centres are not equipped to address. Targeted, one-on-one IB support — from a tutor who knows the assessment criteria, the command terms, and the school's pacing — is what makes a genuine difference here.

For younger students in PYP or MYP, parents in this area frequently want to build strong conceptual foundations early, before the DP pressure arrives. That kind of proactive approach, starting tuition in Grade 6 or 8 rather than Grade 11, is a pattern we see regularly among Saket families.
items: Support across PYP, MYP and DP frameworks
Assessment-aware tutoring, not generic subject revision
Proactive MYP foundation work ahead of DP entry
Flexible scheduling for busy South Delhi school calendars

contentBlocks.heading: IB Programmes Covered: PYP, MYP and Diploma
contentBlocks.body:
IB Gram tutors in Delhi work across all three main school programmes. In the Primary Years Programme, the focus is on inquiry-led learning, concept development and building early numeracy and literacy habits that align with the school's unit-of-inquiry model. Tutors help young learners connect classroom concepts to practice without disrupting the school's approach.

In the Middle Years Programme, the challenge shifts toward managing subject interdependence and the Personal Project in Grade 10. Tutors familiar with the MYP criteria help students understand how assessments are marked — criterion by criterion — rather than relying on guesswork.

The Diploma Programme is where subject-specific expertise matters most. DP tutors in our network understand the difference between Higher Level and Standard Level demands, the role of the Internal Assessment in the overall grade, and the specific deadlines that schools set for first drafts and supervisor check-ins. For the Extended Essay, tutors support research planning and argument structure — the intellectual work stays entirely the student's own. TOK tutors help students understand how knowledge claims are constructed, which is often the part of the DP that surprises students most. Command terms — analyse, evaluate, compare, discuss — are not interchangeable, and experienced tutors make that distinction clear early.
items: PYP inquiry support and concept consolidation
MYP criterion-based assessment guidance
DP HL and SL subject tuition with IA support
EE research framing and TOK argument structure

contentBlocks.heading: Subjects Available for Saket Students
contentBlocks.body:
The most common subject requests from Saket and nearby South Delhi families reflect the DP subject mix at local international schools. Mathematics is consistently the highest-demand area: both Math Analysis and Approaches (AA) at HL and SL, and Math Applications and Interpretation (AI) at HL and SL. Students often need help distinguishing which course suits their target university pathway, and then building consistent problem-solving habits once enrolled.

Physics at HL is another frequent request, particularly for students targeting engineering or medicine in Indian or UK universities. The data-analysis and experimental skills required in the Physics IA are areas where a good tutor adds real structure. Chemistry tutors in our network work with both the conceptual and calculation-heavy elements of the syllabus, including organic chemistry and stoichiometry.

Economics is popular among DP students across South Delhi; tutor support here focuses on correctly applying economic theory to real-world commentaries and practising the specific command terms the examiner expects. English A: Language and Literature or Literature is frequently requested for Paper 1 and Paper 2 exam preparation, as well as the Individual Oral.

Biology is available for both SL and HL. Availability across all subjects varies depending on tutor schedules and student location — we will always be transparent about this during the matching process.
items: Math AA and AI at HL and SL
Physics, Chemistry and Biology HL/SL
Economics commentary and exam-technique support
English A literature analysis and oral preparation

contentBlocks.heading: Home Tuition Logistics and Nearby Areas
contentBlocks.body:
Saket's internal road network is reasonably navigable by auto, cab or two-wheeler, which means home tutors based within 3-5 kilometres can usually manage sessions without the unpredictability that affects more congested Delhi corridors. However, Delhi's traffic patterns shift significantly by time of day, and families should factor in after-school rush between 3:30 and 6:00 PM when planning in-person session windows.

The areas immediately surrounding Saket — Defence Colony, Hauz Khas, Green Park, Panchsheel Park, New Friends Colony and Jor Bagh — form a natural tutoring cluster for South Delhi. Many tutors in our network who are comfortable working in Saket are also available in one or more of these neighbouring localities. This gives families flexibility if they are moving between residences or if a sibling studies in a different part of the cluster.

When a suitable home tutor is not available at the right time or in the right subject, online tuition is a strong practical alternative. Online sessions remove travel altogether, open access to tutors from across Delhi and beyond, and are often easier to schedule for the mid-week sessions that students need before an internal assessment deadline. A hybrid approach — one home session per week for relationship-building, one online session for targeted practice — is a format that works well for many Saket families. For broader Delhi-wide searches, students can also explore tutors serving the wider city through IB Gram's Delhi hub.
items: Tutors reachable across Saket, Defence Colony, Hauz Khas and Green Park
Hybrid scheduling to manage weekday and weekend sessions
Online access to the full Delhi-wide tutor network
City-level fallback when hyper-local availability is limited

contentBlocks.heading: How IB Gram Matches Tutors in Saket
contentBlocks.body:
Matching for Saket students starts with a short academic consultation — either a call or a detailed form — where we capture the programme (PYP, MYP or DP), the specific subject and level (for example, Math AA HL or Economics SL), the student's current grade, the school's upcoming assessment calendar, and the preferred mode of tuition. We also ask about the student's learning style and any previous tuition experience, since a student who has had ineffective tuition before sometimes needs a different kind of tutor rather than just a different subject approach.

For DP students, we pay close attention to IA and EE timelines. If a student is three weeks from a first draft submission, a tutor who can engage quickly and knows the assessment criteria for that specific subject is more valuable than one who is broadly knowledgeable but unfamiliar with the IB format. We will tell you honestly when a match for a very specific need may take a few days to arrange, rather than suggesting someone unsuitable.

Once a candidate tutor is identified, we share their background, subject experience and programme familiarity before any session is booked. You are never committed to continuing if the first session is not the right fit. Adjustments are part of the process, not a sign that something went wrong.
items: Programme, subject, level and grade captured upfront
School assessment calendar used to prioritise urgency
Tutor profile shared before any session commitment
Free to adjust if the first-session fit is not right

contentBlocks.heading: Home, Online or Hybrid: What Works in Saket
contentBlocks.body:
Home tuition in Saket works best when there is a consistent, stable schedule and the student benefits from an in-person environment — particularly younger PYP and MYP learners who need structured, face-to-face engagement to stay focused. Parents often prefer home sessions for primary and middle school students because they can observe the tutor's approach and maintain oversight without sitting in the room.

For DP students, the calculus shifts. Many DP students prefer online sessions because they can share their screen, annotate past papers in real time and access sessions during free periods at school or at a relative's home. The flexibility of online tuition also means a student in Saket can access a specialist in a niche subject — for example, an experienced Math AA HL tutor or a French B HL tutor — who may not be available for home visits in this exact area.

Hybrid is increasingly the format of choice for families who want the best of both: a home session at the start of each week to set the agenda and build the student-tutor relationship, and a shorter online check-in mid-week to review practice and clear doubts before the next school day. This format is practical in Saket given the relatively predictable inner South Delhi commute distances for weekly visits.
items: Home sessions for PYP and MYP relationship and focus
Online for DP niche subjects and schedule flexibility
Hybrid weekly-plus-midweek format popular with DP families
Mode can shift as the student's assessment calendar changes

contentBlocks.heading: How Tutors Are Reviewed and How We Communicate
contentBlocks.body:
Every tutor on IB Gram's platform is reviewed before being listed. We look at their subject background, their familiarity with the specific IB programme they claim to teach, and any prior experience working with IB students at the level they are applying for. Tutors who have completed the IB Diploma as students, or who have tutored multiple cohorts through DP assessments, are typically well-placed to guide students through the nuances of the programme.

We do not promise that every tutor will be the perfect match immediately — tutor-student compatibility matters and it is not always visible until the first session. What we do promise is transparency: we share the tutor's relevant background before commitment, we ask for early feedback after the first or second session, and we are available to help adjust if the fit is not working.

Parent communication is a part of the process, not an afterthought. We encourage tutors to maintain a brief session summary — even a short WhatsApp note — after each session so that parents in Saket can track progress without having to sit in on every meeting. Where parents want a more formal progress update, we can facilitate that through our consultation process.
items: Programme and subject background reviewed before listing
Early feedback requested after initial sessions
Tutor profile shared transparently before booking
Regular parent communication encouraged through tutors

contentBlocks.heading: School Context for Saket and Nearby South Delhi
contentBlocks.body:
Several international and internationally-minded schools are associated with the wider South Delhi area. DPS International Saket follows an international curriculum framework and is among the schools whose students and families approach IB Gram for supplementary support. The British School New Delhi and the American Embassy School, both well-established international schools in Delhi, draw students and families from across South and Central Delhi, including Saket and its surrounding areas.

Families from these schools, and others in the South Delhi corridor, often seek IB-specific tuition because the curriculum's demands differ meaningfully from CBSE or ICSE preparation. Schools typically provide internal deadlines, criterion sheets and subject guides — but classroom time alone does not always allow for the depth of one-on-one practice that a student needs for their IA or exam preparation.

IB Gram is an independent tutoring platform and is not officially affiliated with these schools unless specifically stated. Mentioning a school by name reflects the geographic reality that students from those schools may seek support from tutors in and around Saket — it does not imply any formal endorsement, partnership or authorisation from any school or its leadership.
items: DPS International Saket — an IB-framework school in the area
British School New Delhi and American Embassy School students across South Delhi
Independent support for students from any school, CBSE or international
No affiliation claimed; school names reflect geography only

contentBlocks.heading: Honest Notes on Availability and Academic Integrity
contentBlocks.body:
Availability in Saket — as in any Delhi locality — is not guaranteed in advance for every subject and every time slot. When we say we will help you find a tutor, we mean we will search our active network and be honest with you about what is feasible. If a specific subject at a specific level has limited tutor options in your area right now, we will tell you rather than match you with someone who is not a good fit.

Academic integrity matters to us and it should matter to parents too. IB tutors on our platform are there to guide thinking, model good analytical habits, explain difficult concepts and help students practise — they do not write Internal Assessments, Extended Essays or TOK essays for students. Assessed work submitted to the school must reflect the student's own thinking and writing. A good tutor helps a student get there independently; they do not shortcut the process. Families who understand this distinction tend to see the best outcomes from tuition, because the student's own capability grows rather than a dependency forming.

We also do not make guarantees about grades, predicted scores or university admissions. Tuition improves understanding and builds confidence — what a student does with that is their own achievement.
items: Availability stated honestly based on real network capacity
No guarantees of marks, grades or exam outcomes
IA, EE and TOK remain fully student-owned work
Tutors guide and practise — they do not complete assessed work

contentBlocks.heading: Start Your Search for an IB Tutor in Saket
contentBlocks.body:
If you are a parent in Saket or a nearby South Delhi area looking for IB tuition — whether for a PYP child just getting started, an MYP student building toward DP, or a Diploma student under assessment pressure — the next step is a short conversation. There is no commitment involved. You tell us the programme, subject, level and what you are trying to address, and we tell you honestly what options look like given our current network and tutor availability.

We are not a tuition centre with a fixed roster. We work to match families with tutors who fit the specific need — the subject, the level, the school's assessment calendar and the student's learning style. Some matches come together quickly; some take a few days to arrange correctly. We prefer to take that time and get it right rather than suggest a tutor who is not suited to IB.

Book a free academic consultation through IB Gram. If you have questions before booking, you are welcome to reach out directly. We work with families across Saket, Defence Colony, Hauz Khas, New Friends Colony, Panchsheel Park, Green Park and the wider South Delhi area.
items: Free academic consultation — no commitment to book
Honest assessment of tutor availability in your area
Serving Saket, Defence Colony, Hauz Khas and nearby South Delhi
PYP, MYP and DP — all levels, home and online

faqs.question: Can I find an IB home tutor who will come to Saket for sessions?
faqs.answer: Yes, home tuition in Saket is possible, and tutors within South Delhi's inner belt can often manage in-person visits here. That said, availability depends on the specific subject, level, tutor schedule and your exact location within Saket. We will assess this honestly during the matching process and suggest online or hybrid options if in-person is not feasible right away.

faqs.question: My child attends DPS International Saket. Can a tutor align with the school's IB curriculum schedule?
faqs.answer: Tutors on IB Gram are familiar with the IB programme structure and can work with whatever assessment timeline, criterion sheet or subject guide your child's school provides. IB Gram is an independent platform and is not affiliated with DPS International Saket, but that does not prevent a tutor from being well-aligned with your child's specific deadlines and course requirements.

faqs.question: What IB subjects are most commonly available for students in South Delhi?
faqs.answer: Math AA and AI at both HL and SL are consistently among the most-requested and most-available subjects in our Delhi network. Physics, Chemistry, Biology, Economics and English A are also regularly available. Availability in any specific subject can vary by tutor schedule and student location — we will tell you what is realistic for your situation during the consultation.

faqs.question: Is online IB tuition as effective as home tuition for a DP student?
faqs.answer: For many DP students, online tuition works very well. Shared screens, annotated past papers and recorded session links are practical tools that in-person sessions do not easily replicate. Online also removes commute uncertainty, which matters when a student has an IA deadline approaching. The right mode depends on the student, the subject and the stage of the academic year — we can help you think through this.

faqs.question: Can a tutor help my child with their Extended Essay or Internal Assessment?
faqs.answer: Tutors can support the research planning, argument framing and structural thinking that goes into an EE or IA — but the intellectual content, the writing and the final submission must be the student's own work. IB schools take academic integrity seriously, and so do we. A tutor's role is to build the student's capability and confidence, not to produce assessed work on their behalf.

faqs.question: Is IB Gram officially affiliated with The British School New Delhi or the American Embassy School?
faqs.answer: No. IB Gram is an independent tutoring platform and is not officially affiliated with these schools unless specifically stated. We mention them because students and families from international schools across South Delhi, including those near Saket, regularly seek independent IB tuition support. Our work is separate from any school's official programme, staff or endorsements.

finalCta: Book a free academic consultation with IB Gram to discuss your child's IB programme, subject needs and location — and find out what tutor options are realistically available in and around Saket, South Delhi.
